author: octocat
headline: Cache layer learns to forget what it never needed to know
standfirst: A cleanup that should have happened a year ago, told in two paragraphs and a tidy diff.
body:
The pull request strips an entire branch of dead caching logic that hasn't been read from since the 2025 schema migration. What was once a sophisticated multi-tier eviction policy is now four lines and a comment.

Reviewers noted the change with relief. Tests pass. Latency on the affected endpoint drops by a measurable but undramatic margin — the kind of quiet win that doesn't make changelogs but is exactly why people read the changelog twice.
